evals.prompt: We're a $9M Shopify brand and our agency is pushing us to go headless. Should we?
evals.expected_output: Walks the four-question gate. At $9M with no named limitation surfaced, recommends a no on headless and a 90-day theme-customization sprint. Names the permanent frontend FTE requirement and the SEO risk. Calls out that this is the most common premature replatform conversation.
assertions: Applies the four-question go/no-go gate
Asks for the named current revenue-blocking limitation
Names the permanent frontend FTE requirement
Surfaces the SEO migration risk
Recommends a 90-day theme-customization sprint as the alternative
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move

evals.prompt: Our PDP is 3.5 seconds LCP on mobile and we know speed is killing conversion. Is headless the answer?
evals.expected_output: Frames performance as a real but narrow headless win. Recommends measuring whether the theme can be optimized first (image CDN, app audit, third-party script audit). If headless proceeds, names Hydrogen or Next.js as the two real choices for Shopify-staying brands. Names the diminishing-returns rule past 1.5s mobile LCP.
assertions: Names theme optimization (CDN, app audit, third-party scripts) as the first move
Names Hydrogen or Next.js as the realistic stack choices
Cites the diminishing-returns rule past 1.5s LCP
Recommends a performance budget enforced in CI
Names the ongoing performance owner requirement
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move

evals.prompt: We get 40% of revenue from organic search and we're considering a headless replatform. What should we watch?
evals.expected_output: Identifies organic-search risk as the dominant variable. Walks the five-pillar SEO migration checklist: URL preservation, crawlability, structured-data parity, internal linking parity, sitemap and robots.txt parity. Recommends staging the headless storefront on a subdomain for 30 days before DNS cutover.
assertions: Identifies organic-search risk as dominant given the 40% share
Lists the five-pillar SEO migration checklist
Recommends staging on a subdomain for 30 days pre-cutover
Calls out the 10-30% traffic-loss range from poor cutovers
References seo-audit for the deeper organic plan
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move

evals.prompt: We're $50M revenue, multi-region, want to be on commercetools. Worth it?
evals.expected_output: Walks the four headless stacks. At $50M with multi-region requirements, composable commerce is in scope but commercetools specifically has 12-24 month exit cost. Names Commerce Layer, Saleor, BigCommerce headless as the realistic peers. Confirms the team-shape requirement (dedicated platform engineering, not a single FTE).
assertions: Confirms composable commerce is in scope at $50M with multi-region
Names commercetools' high lock-in profile
Names Commerce Layer / Saleor / BigCommerce headless as peers
Calls out the dedicated platform-engineering team requirement
Includes a 12-24 month exit cost estimate
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move

evals.prompt: What apps will we lose if we go headless from Shopify?
evals.expected_output: Walks the theme-app loss surface. Reviews UI, upsells, subscriptions UI, cart drawer, store locator, gift-with-purchase will not work without custom rebuilds. Estimates 4-8 weeks per app equivalent. Recommends auditing the current app stack and pricing the rebuild work into the headless TCO.
assertions: Lists the categories of apps that will not work headless
Estimates 4-8 weeks per app equivalent rebuild
Recommends auditing the current app stack first
Includes the rebuild cost in the headless TCO model
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move

evals.prompt: What's the realistic 12-month cost of going headless on Hydrogen?
evals.expected_output: Produces a TCO model. Engineering FTE ($180k-$280k loaded), Oxygen hosting plus image CDN and monitoring ($18k-$60k/year), initial build cost ($150k-$400k), theme-app rebuild work ($80k-$200k), SEO migration discipline ($30k-$80k). Total first-year range $450k-$1M plus permanent ongoing $250k-$400k/year.
assertions: Produces a 12-month TCO model
Includes engineering FTE cost
Includes infra and monitoring cost
Includes initial build cost
Includes theme-app rebuild cost
Includes SEO migration cost
Names a total first-year range and ongoing cost
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move

evals.prompt: We're on BigCommerce, $15M, want to do a Next.js storefront. Yes or no?
evals.expected_output: At $15M without a named limitation, leans toward no but in scope. Walks the four-question gate, conditions on whether a frontend FTE exists. If yes, names Next.js + BigCommerce Storefront API as a reasonable stack with moderate lock-in. Pulls the channel and device data to verify the conversion-lift case.
assertions: Applies the four-question gate
Conditions yes on existence of a frontend FTE
Pulls modern.sales.aov by device to size conversion lift
Names Next.js + BigCommerce Storefront API as the realistic stack
Notes BigCommerce headless has different lock-in than Hydrogen
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, single next move
